HIGH TICKET LEAD GEN SALES FUNNEL
This bonus video was created for a client to explain how we built their FB ads funnel,
predominantly to explain to them what kind of creative we were after and why.
This is one of many funnels for lead gen, but is targeted at products $2K+ in value, with
close being completed via a phone call.
However the FB ads funnel itself can still be used for mid level items $500+ as well.
00:00 / 0:00
Additional notes:
- Use Video Views for COLD audiences to create an engaged audience to retarget in your WARM
campaigns
- For COLD use product and brand videography that does NOT sell. Make it packed full of value
- If you're wondering why not go for the freebie in COLD; there's no reason to suggest it won't work,
but higher ticket purchase often need more touch points to create an audience that's highly
relevant. Giving away a free PDF or webinar without getting them bought in may reduce quality and
increase cost per lead
- It doesn't have to be videos in COLD. You can also run normal ads (Page Post Engagement
objective) and use post engagement to deliver your WARM audience
- The WARM audience set is solely a remarketing audience based on engagement in COLD. Do you
need this step? Yes, because this funnel is building trust and intrigue. Where COLD was the hook,
WARM is now the line pulling the fish out the water
- Should you only limit budget at 80%, 10%, 10%? No. This was an illustration for the client, but for
you it will depend on your audience sizes through the funnel. It's quite feasible that WARM could be
15-20% for example
- Your HOT audience is basic site retargeting. For this funnel we setup Lead as the pixel event fired
when a user signed up for the free PDF on the site and CompleteRegistration for those that had
gone to the next step and made a booking
CAN I USE THIS FUNNEL FOR A WEBINAR INSTEAD OF PDF STYLE LEAD MAGNET?
- If the value of the product is $500+ (as a loose benchmark) then YES. Replace the first step of the
funnel (Opt in) with a Webinar opt-in. Tip: what reportedly works best is an evergreen webinar
(recorded once then played on repeat) available in 15 minute intervals. Previously it was
recommended to run this a few times a day if not hourly but since our attention spans have
dropped you need to get them in ASAP. If you're running the webinar live then it will likely be
limited on your availability.
- Below $500 that then I would instead use a more direct funnel:
1) Great value PDF/giveaway in exchange for email
2) Email sequence to indoctrinate then sell the product mirrored by Facebook Ads
3) Retargeting for those that have visited the site not purchased or visited not signed up for PDF
- The funnel length and complexity generally matches the ticket value because of the decision
process involved in buying something for $200 vs $2000 for example
CAN I USE THIS FUNNEL FOR ECOMMERCE
- YES! Especially, again for higher ticket items, but it does depend on what type of product you are
selling. A client of mine selling furniture pieces ranging from £250-£2000 with an AOV of around £450
have a funnel which is:
1) COLD audience prospecting using a Website Conversion campaign with carousels and videos to
showcase how the client's products could enhance their living space
2) WARM audience retargeting using Website Conversion targeting watched/engaged people with
direct response ads showcasing individual products in a carousel to entice them in to click
3) HOT audience retargeting using both Dynamic Product Ads and a Website Conversion campaign
with static images and aiming to break down barriers to buying/ pushing scarcity as they run on
limited stock
- However typically for most Ecom the path is shorter and less complicated as you'll see in the same
video for Ecommerce
